Title: Recreating the synthesis of starch granules in yeast

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: intracellular granule-like particles in yeast cells
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of insoluble starch-like glucans/granules; without ISA, most glucans remain soluble, but in some BE3-only backgrounds insoluble glucans can still form.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in glycogen-free yeast; systematic combinatorial strain library; enzyme activity zymogram; glucan fractionation/quantification; iodine staining; microscopy; CLD profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ISA1 functions together with ISA2 as the active isoamylase complex in yeast. 2. Presence of ISA is the major driver of insoluble glucan/starch-like granule formation. 3. ISA expression also generates linear malto-oligosaccharides, consistent with branch hydrolysis during glucan maturation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ISA1 alone was not treated as active; ISA1 and ISA2 were introduced together as ISA heteromultimer. Although essential in most cases, insoluble glucans could exceptionally arise without ISA in some low-branching BE3-only combinations.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: intracellular granule-like particles in yeast cells
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required together with ISA1 for active isoamylase that promotes insoluble glucan formation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous co-expression with ISA1 in glycogen-free yeast; enzyme activity zymogram; glucan profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ISA2 partners with ISA1 to form the active ISA heteromultimer. 2. The ISA complex is a key determinant shifting glucans from soluble to insoluble starch-like forms. 3. Its effects are context dependent on starch synthase composition.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Treated experimentally as obligate partner of ISA1; not independently assayed as a standalone catalytic factor.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: elongated alpha-glucan chains enriched in short chains (DP 6-10)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from ADP-glucose to non-reducing ends of alpha-glucan chains to elongate starch polymers.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly elongates amylopectin chains that contribute to final starch-like glucans
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: intracellular granule-like particles in yeast cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: As sole synthase produces glucans, but less efficiently in glycogenin-free yeast; consistently increases short chains (DP 6-10) and decreases relative insolubility of glucans when ISA is present.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression as sole synthase and in combinatorial libraries; zymogram; glucan quantification; CLD by HPAEC-PAD; microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: galactose induction in yeast expression system
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SS1 is enzymatically active in yeast and can synthesize glucans as sole synthase. 2. Compared with other SS isoforms, SS1 gives low glucan accumulation in glycogenin-free background, suggesting weaker primer utilization/initiation. 3. SS1 increases short chains (DP 6-10) and opposes insoluble starch-like glucan formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for tuning amylopectin fine structure toward shorter chains, but likely should be balanced against other synthases if insoluble granule formation is desired.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promotes soluble rather than insoluble glucans and may reduce granule crystallinity/insolubility.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Glucan synthesis by SS1 was strongly improved when endogenous yeast glycogenins were present, implying limited intrinsic priming capability.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from ADP-glucose to elongate amylopectin chains, preferentially generating intermediate-length chains.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly elongates glucan chains contributing to starch-like product architecture
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: intracellular granule-like particles in yeast cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces glucans as sole synthase; consistently increases intermediate-length chains; promotes insoluble glucans mainly when SS4 is absent.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression; codon-optimized construct; zymogram; Western blot; glucan quantification; CLD by HPAEC-PAD
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SS2 is active in yeast and retains its chain-elongation specificity, increasing intermediate-length chains. 2. Its positive effect on insoluble glucan formation is conditional and most evident in the absence of SS4. 3. Slight dependence on primers/glycogenins was suggested.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A useful engineering target to tune intermediate amylopectin chain lengths and potentially improve granule formation depending on synthase balance.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Effect on insoluble glucan formation is context dependent and may be masked by SS4 or enzyme dosage imbalance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A codon-optimized sequence for yeast was required because the native coding sequence was not expressed otherwise.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: elongated alpha-glucan chains, tending toward intermediate/longer chains in some contexts
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from ADP-glucose to elongate amylopectin glucan chains.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly elongates chains contributing to starch-like granule structure
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: intracellular granule-like particles in yeast cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports glucan synthesis as sole synthase; in absence of SS2 tends to increase intermediate-length chains; required with SS4-related contexts for efficient glucan accumulation when ISA is present.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression; zymogram; glucan quantification; CLD analysis; combinatorial strain comparisons
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SS3 is active in yeast and can synthesize glucans alone. 2. It does not show a strong distinct signature on short/intermediate chains overall, but in the absence of SS2 it tends to increase intermediate-length chains. 3. Together with other synthases it contributes to insoluble glucan formation in a context-dependent way.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be combined with other synthases to modulate chain-length profile and support granule biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No uniform effect on insoluble glucans; may contribute differently depending on other synthase isoforms and branching context.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SS3 activity appeared elevated in yeast relative to Arabidopsis according to zymograms.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from ADP-glucose to elongate starch glucan chains.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly contributes to architecture enabling insoluble starch-like granule formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: intracellular granule-like particles in yeast cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Consistently and strongly promotes formation of insoluble glucans when ISA is present, despite no clear distinct CLD signature.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression; zymogram; tagged protein immunoblot; glucan quantification; combinatorial strain comparisons; microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SS4 is active in yeast and can synthesize glucans alone. 2. Among the SS isoforms, SS4 most consistently promotes insoluble starch-like glucan formation. 3. Its effect is not explained by a clear chain-length signature, suggesting influence on structural features not fully captured by CLD analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for insoluble granule formation in this reconstituted system
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A high-value engineering target for improving starch granule formation and insolubility in reconstructed or crop systems.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Its mechanism is incompletely understood; excessive or imbalanced activity relative to other enzymes may alter native structure.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper discusses a priming/initiation-related role for SS4 based on broader Arabidopsis genetics, but in this yeast study the direct demonstrated effect is promotion of insoluble glucan formation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces alpha-1,6 branch points into alpha-1,4 glucan chains by cleaving and transferring glucan segments.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required to build branched amylopectin-like polymers that can mature into starch-like granules
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: intracellular granule-like particles in yeast cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functionally complements yeast branching deficiency and supports high glucan production; together with BE3 shapes solubility and structure of final glucans.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single-enzyme expression in branching-deficient yeast; native zymogram; glucan quantification in combinatorial lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BE2 is enzymatically active in yeast and enables substantial glucan accumulation in the absence of endogenous yeast branching enzyme. 2. Together with BE3 it supports synthesis of soluble and insoluble amylopectin-like glucans. 3. Branching enzyme composition strongly influences glucan insolubility and iodine spectral properties.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for branch pattern, but not individually established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for modulating branch density and thereby starch functionality in synthetic or crop systems.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered branching balance relative to synthases may shift products toward abnormal chain-length distributions and soluble glucans.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Both BE2 and BE3 were usually kept constant in the main 30-line library.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces alpha-1,6 branches into alpha-1,4 glucan chains.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required to create branched amylopectin-like substrate for starch-like granule formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: intracellular granule-like particles in yeast cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports high glucan production and, when present alone in some genetic backgrounds, can allow insoluble glucan formation even without ISA, implying lower branching can favor insolubility in specific contexts.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single-enzyme expression in branching-deficient yeast; zymogram; combinatorial strain comparisons; glucan quantification; iodine spectral analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BE3 is active in yeast and enables substantial glucan accumulation. 2. BE3-only backgrounds can produce insoluble glucans in selected strains even without isoamylase. 3. BE composition alters glucan structure and shifts iodine absorption toward longer wavelengths.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for branch pattern, but not individually established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to lower effective branching and promote crystallization/alter starch structure, but effects are highly context dependent.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: BE3-only conditions can reduce total glucan content in some backgrounds and create non-native structures.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper interprets BE3-only effects mainly as altered overall branching level rather than unique BE3 specificity.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: intracellular granule-like particles in yeast cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides ADP-glucose required for operation of the plant starch-biosynthetic pathway in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression with HA-tag detection; pathway reconstitution in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A deregulated E. coli AGPase was introduced because yeast glycogen metabolism uses UDP-glucose whereas plant starch synthesis requires ADP-glucose. 2. GlgC-TM supports reconstitution of the Arabidopsis starch pathway in yeast by supplying ADP-glucose. 3. It is a necessary upstream enabling component rather than the main variable tested in structure-function comparisons.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful enabling module for heterologous starch engineering in non-plant hosts or for boosting ADP-glucose supply.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains R67K, P295D, and G336D substitutions rendering it insensitive to allosteric regulation.
